# Agentic Market Intelligence Review

Status: reviewed  
Owner: ProductOS PM  
Audience: PM Builder, PM Leader / Communicator  
Updated at: 2026-03-23  
Source artifact ids: `research_notebook_agentic_market_intelligence`, `landscape_matrix_agentic_market_intelligence`, `competitor_dossier_agentic_market_intelligence`, `market_analysis_brief_agentic_market_intelligence`

## Summary

The current market is no longer centered on basic AI drafting. Direct PM tools, deep-research products, and CI platforms are all moving toward some combination of grounded context, recurring refresh, and more visible control over what the AI is allowed to use and decide.

## What The Market Already Has

- Direct PM tools such as Productboard Spark and Jira Product Discovery are bringing AI into the PM workflow itself.
- Adjacent platforms such as Notion, Coda, and Asana are making connected context and agent-style teammates normal.
- Deep-research products from OpenAI, Google, and Perplexity make plan-and-execute research with report output feel standard.
- CI tools such as Crayon, Klue, and Semrush normalize watchlists, deltas, and recurring refresh.

## ProductOS Implication

ProductOS should not compete by copying generic drafting or one-shot summaries.

ProductOS should compete by combining:

- PM-native structured state
- source-tier visibility
- freshness and contradiction controls
- bounded auto-research loops
- Ralph-based review and loophole memory

## Current Guardrails

The current slice can now support unattended recurring market refresh under explicit freshness, contradiction, and escalation guardrails.

The current governed distribution proof covers an internal leadership digest and portfolio-safe summary path.

The remaining boundary is external publication adapters and broader packaging, not refresh safety or internal distribution readiness.
